in_scope_assets: Latest stable release v4.x (Go file-listing server, AGPL-3.0)
out_of_scope: OpenListApp/OpenListApp and any other third-party derivative or fork projects
Paid proprietary software using the OpenList name
Older (non-latest-stable) versions — only latest stable v4.x receives security patches

Underlying third-party storage providers and their SDKs/APIs (Aliyundrive, OneDrive, GoogleDrive, S3, Dropbox, WebDAV remotes, Mega, BaiduNetdisk, etc.) — OpenList only performs HTTP 302 redirects / traffic forwarding without modification
Cloudflare-hosted documentation and demo sites (doc.oplist.org, doc.openlist.team, doc.oplist.org.cn, demo.oplist.org, demo.oplist.org.cn) unless the vulnerability is in OpenList source itself
Misconfiguration in user-operated deployments (weak admin password, plain HTTP exposure, container running as root) — covered by user-side best-practices
severity_ratings: Not formally published. Standard CVSS-style triage by maintainers expected via GitHub Private Security Advisory.
reward_range: No monetary rewards — community/open-source project. Acknowledgment in SECURITY.md credits only.
notes: OpenList is a community-driven AGPL-3.0 fork of AList (originally by Xhofe / AlistGo/alist). There is no formal bug bounty program. Vulnerability reports must be submitted privately via GitHub Security Advisory (https://github.com/OpenListTeam/OpenList/security/advisories/new) or Telegram @OpenListTeam — public Issues for security disclosures are explicitly forbidden by SECURITY.md. The project is a Go-based file-listing/proxy server supporting many cloud storage backends, WebDAV, web upload, offline download, and Cloudflare Workers proxy. No smart contracts or on-chain components — strictly a server-side application.
